metadescription.value: Through the Rolex mentoring programme, mentor Anne Lacaton and architect Arine Aprahamian collaborated on an urban renewal project in Beirut. Watch the video on rolex.org.

text.value: In 2023–2024, through the Rolex mentoring programme, Lebanese-Armenian architect Arine Aprahamian was guided by French Pritzker Prize-winning architect Anne Lacaton as she conducted a study of Beirut’s Bourj Hammoud neighbourhood, where she grew up. In a place where space is scarce and public areas are nearly absent, she examines how small, strategic changes can improve quality of life. Mentoring forms part of Rolex’s commitment to the transmission of knowledge to the next generation. It takes place alongside its support for leading arts institutions and artists around the world through the Perpetual Arts Initiative.

pageTitle.value: Peter Zumthor and Gloria Cabral - Video

pageCaption.value: Gloria Cabral, from Asunción in Paraguay, spent weeks at the Swiss studio of her mentor Peter Zumthor, who involved her in the whole process of building a tea chapel in South Korea, opening up her mind “in a radical way”.

pageTitle.value: Kazuyo Sejima and Yang Zhao - Video

pageCaption.value: Japanese mentor Kazuyo Sejima and protégé Yang Zhao believe architecture is about more than constructing buildings – it’s about changing lives. They put their conviction into practice with a humanitarian project as Zhao designs a community gathering place in a tsunami-devastated area.

pageTitle.value: Sir David Chipperfield and Simon Kretz - Video

pageCaption.value: For Sir David Chipperfield and his Swiss protégé Simon Kretz, the mentoring year provided the perfect opportunity for a detailed examination of the impact of architecture on society – how buildings can create strong communities if they relate to people and the environment.

pageTitle.value: Bernardine Evaristo & Ayesha Harruna Attah - Video

pageCaption.value: Author of five novels, Ghanaian writer Ayesha Harruna Attah wants to write African stories for Africans and for her books to reach a wider audience. Booker Prize-winning author Bernardine Evaristo offered advice on how to build an international career.

WaPageTitle.value: Bernardine Evaristo & Ayesha Harruna Attah - Video

metadescription.value: Through the Rolex mentoring programme, mentor Bernardine Evaristo is helping author Ayesha Harruna Attah gain wider readership. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Dianne Reeves & Song Yi Jeon - Video

pageCaption.value: South Korean jazz singer and composer Song Yi Jeon, who is known for her hypnotic voice, marked the end of her two-year collaboration with five-time Grammy winner Dianne Reeves through the Rolex mentoring programme by performing at the 2024 EFG London Jazz Festival.

metadescription.value: Through the Rolex mentoring programme, jazz singer Dianne Reeves is helping jazz composer Song Yi Jeon grow confident in her art. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Convinced that the goal of architecture is to transform, British-Ghanaian architecture mentor Sir David Adjaye and his protégée Mariam Issoufou from Niger set about producing a concrete result for social good, with Issoufou designing a major cultural complex for her country’s capital, Niamey.

metadescription.value: Mentor David Adjaye and his protégée Mariam Issoufou are using architecture for social good in Niger’s capital. Watch the video on rolex.org.
